workstream.name: ts2pant General-Loop SSA
workstream.unlocks: General `while` support. L5 (break/continue) can extend the loop-body machinery, and L6 (loop-summary unification) can target both bounded (L2/L3) and fixed-point (L4) shapes via the same `IR1SsaLoopBody` discriminator on `terminationMetric`.

problemStatement: After L3, the mutating-body path lowers bounded counter loops spelled as either `for` (L2) or `let`+`while` (L3) to quantified over-each equations. Unbounded `while` loops — those without a recognized counter-shaped variant — still reject with the diagnostic `"while loop is not a recognized bounded-counter shape; lift to L4 fixed-point lowering when that milestone ships"` (translate-body.ts:4496). Real TS code routinely writes loops whose termination depends on dynamic predicate evaluation (`while (a.balance < target)`, `while (s.has(i))`-style searches that mutate `s`, predicate-driven state-machine steps) that L3's ranking-function extraction cannot recover. These need a fundamentally different Pant target — a recursive rule definition expressing the loop's post-state as a fixed point of the body's iteration — and a Pant SMT backend that emits `define-fun-rec` so the recursive definition is solver-visible.
solutionSummary: Lower unbounded `while` loops to a synthesised top-level recursive Pant rule. The mutating-body builder constructs `ir1While(cond, body)` for two routes: (a) `let`+`while` pairs that fail L3's bounded-counter recognition fall through to L4 instead of rejecting; (b) bare `while (P) { body }` without a preceding let routes directly to L4. A new module `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` consumes the `ir1While` form and produces an `IR1SsaProgram` whose `IR1SsaLoopBody.terminationMetric` is null (the L1 fixed-point discriminant), plus two propositions: a top-level recursive rule declaration `fn--loop-N`, and the primed-equation caller `loc' obj = fn--loop-N (loc obj) ...invariants`. Pant's SMT emitter (`lib/smt.ml`) detects rules that syntactically reference themselves in their body and emits `(define-fun-rec ...)` instead of the existing `(declare-fun ...) + (assert (forall ...))` shape, so Z3/CVC5's recursive-function induction machinery handles `pant --check` on the lowered output. `while (true)` literal-true loops reject at the build pass with a targeted "no observable termination condition" diagnostic. Scope is one mutated property per loop; multi-location loops are out of scope and continue to reject.
currentStateAnalysis: L3 shipped in `ts2pant-bounded-while-lowering` (commits `4a2238b`, `8955f11`, `af05feb`, `522cf01`). `tools/ts2pant/src/translate-body.ts:4341-4374` is the let-then-while peephole that calls `buildL1LetWhileMutation`; on null return, the bare `let` falls through to the existing rejection. `tools/ts2pant/src/translate-body.ts:4493-4498` is the catch-all `WhileStatement` reject that emits the L4 pointer message. `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts` recognizes ascending and descending bounded shapes; on failure, callers fall through. `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-lower-body.ts:114` dispatches `kind: "for"` to `lowerCounterLoopL1Body`; there is no `kind: "while"` arm — `ir1While` exists as L1 vocabulary (used today only by the μ-search pure-path lowering in `ir1-lower.ts`) but the mutating-body lowering rejects it. The IR1 SSA loop body vocabulary from L1 (`IR1SsaLoopBody` with `terminationMetric: IR1SsaTerminationMetric | null`, `ir1SsaOpenLoopHeader` / `ir1SsaCloseLoopHeader`) is intentionally bounded/fixed-point-agnostic — null metric is the fixed-point discriminant L4 will use. Pant's SMT emitter (`lib/smt.ml`, `lib/smt_preamble.ml:165`) currently emits every rule as `(declare-fun NAME (ARGS) RET)` plus `(assert (forall ((args …)) (= (NAME args) BODY)))`. Recursive rule bodies are syntactically representable in Pant (e.g., `samples/07-pantagruel.pant` exercises self-referential rule definitions), but the universally-quantified-axiom encoding gives SMT solvers no induction hook — they handle recursion via quantifier instantiation only.

operationalConsiderations.externalSystemAccess: Not applicable. ts2pant is a build-time translator; Pant is its compile target. Neither newly depends on an external system in this gameplan. The `pant --check` SMT solver (Z3 or CVC5) is already in use across the existing fixture corpus; L4 changes the emitted SMT shape but not the dispatch path.
operationalConsiderations.crossRuntimeContracts: The ts2pant→Pant contract changes on both sides in this gameplan: ts2pant emits recursive Pant rule definitions (new emission shape from `ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`), and Pant's SMT backend detects recursive rules and emits `(define-fun-rec ...)` (Patch 2). Both sides ship together. No third runtime sees Pant SMT output. The contract is `pant <fixture.pant>` returns exit 0 (parse + typecheck succeeds) for every passing fixture, and `pant --check <fixture.pant>` returns either sat with a documented model, unsat with a documented reason, or a documented timeout — per the L4 DoD.
operationalConsiderations.failureBehavior: Three failure paths the gameplan handles deliberately. (a) `while (true)` literal-true loops with no observable post-condition reject at the build pass with `"while loop has no observable termination condition (literal-true guard); rewrite with a guard that depends on mutated state"`. The recognizer detects literal-true via L1 expression inspection (`kind: "lit"` with `kind: "bool", value: true`) plus post-canonicalization const-folding chains where the const inlines to true. (b) Multi-location loops (the body modifies more than one rule) reject with `"fixed-point while lowering supports single-location bodies only; this loop modifies N rules"` — the helper's recursive rule signature can only express one return type at this milestone. (c) Pant SMT solver timeouts on `pant --check` per-fixture: the gameplan documents per-fixture acceptable timeouts in the fixture file's JSDoc. The CI integration step (Patch 4) configures a default `--check` timeout (3000ms) and lists fixtures that fall outside that envelope in a `KNOWN_SLOW_CHECKS` constant with explicit per-fixture rationale (per L4 DoD).
operationalConsiderations.concurrencyAndIdempotency: Not applicable. ts2pant is single-threaded and processes one source file at a time; Pant is single-threaded per file. The synthesised recursive-rule name comes from the document-wide `NameRegistry` (existing — see `tools/ts2pant/src/name-registry.ts`) which provides collision-suffixed unique names, so two fixed-point loops in the same source file get distinct helper-rule identifiers without coordination.
operationalConsiderations.rollbackStrategy: Forward-only. The gameplan is additive across all five patches: new module (`ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`), additive recognizer arms in `translate-body.ts`, additive `kind: "while"` arm in `ir1-lower-body.ts`, additive recursive-rule detector in `lib/smt.ml`, additive fixtures + docs. `git revert` of the patches in reverse order returns to L3's state. Pant snapshot tests gate against unintended drift on existing fixtures (no existing fixture should produce different `(declare-fun ...)` vs `(define-fun-rec ...)` shape unless the rule was already recursive — and where it was, the verification outcome may change but the syntactic Pant source is unchanged).

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flag. The gameplan changes a translator's emitted shape (ts2pant adds a new lowering target; Pant adds a new SMT emission for recursive rules); toggling at runtime would expand surface without value. Patch 2 (Pant SMT emitter) is BEHAVIOR but minimal — it affects only rules that syntactically reference themselves, and the new `define-fun-rec` encoding is logically equivalent to the prior `declare-fun + assert (forall ...)` shape (just better-supported by solver induction). Existing Pant fixtures whose rules are recursive (e.g., `samples/07-pantagruel.pant`) get the new encoding; snapshot tests under `test/snapshots/` confirm the parsed-Pant document is unchanged and any SMT-text snapshots are updated as part of Patch 2.
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Sequential with one parallel pair. Patch 1 (INFRA) lands skipped test stubs naming Patches 2-4. Patches 2 (Pant SMT emitter) and 3 (ts2pant fixed-point lowerer module) are INFRA and can land in parallel — they touch different runtimes and have no inter-dependency. Patch 4 (BEHAVIOR) is the single behavior-change patch: wires the build path to construct `ir1While` for the two L4 routes, wires the lower path to dispatch through Patch 3's helper, and lands the three passing fixtures plus the reject fixture. Patch 5 (INFRA) is documentation.

requiredChanges.description: Create the unit test file with `it.skip` stubs for the fixed-point lowerer's recognizer + emitter behaviors: single-location body classification, recursive-rule helper synthesis, the IR1SsaLoopBody construction with null terminationMetric, literal-true rejection, multi-location rejection. Each stub names its implementing patch in a PENDING comment.
Add `it.skip` stubs for the build path's fixed-point routes: let-then-while fall-through builds `ir1While`; bare while builds `ir1While`; literal-true while rejects.

Add pending OCaml unit tests for the Pant SMT emitter's recursive-rule detection: a rule whose body's RHS contains an application of the same rule emits `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)`; a non-recursive rule continues to emit `(declare-fun ...)` + `(assert (forall ...))`. Use `[@tags "pending"]` until Patch 2 lands.

Add `is_rule_recursive : Ast.declaration -> bool` helper that walks the rule body and returns true when the body's expression tree contains an application whose head is the rule's own name. Extend the rule-emission path to dispatch on this predicate: recursive rules emit `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)`; non-recursive rules emit the existing `(declare-fun ...) + (assert (forall ...))` shape. The body expression in the recursive case must be the bare RHS — no quantifier wrapping, since `define-fun-rec` implicitly quantifies over the formal params.
requiredChanges.signature:
val is_rule_recursive : Ast.declaration -> bool

val emit_rule_recursive : Smt_doc.env -> Ast.declaration -> string

requiredChanges.description: Update the rule-declaration emission to dispatch through `Smt.is_rule_recursive` when the rule has a body. Recursive rules skip the `(declare-fun NAME …)` line (define-fun-rec subsumes it). Non-recursive rules keep the existing emission unchanged.

New module mirroring `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts` but for fixed-point loops. Recognizes a `kind: "while"` IR1Stmt with a single mutated location and a non-literal guard. Synthesises a top-level recursive Pant rule via the document-wide `NameRegistry` (`fn--loop-N` pattern with collision suffixing). Constructs `IR1SsaLoopBody` with `terminationMetric: null`. Returns `IR1SsaBodyLowerResult` with two propositions: a `kind: "rule-decl"` for the synthesised recursive helper, and a `kind: "equation"` for the primed-location caller.

Extend `PropResult` with a new variant `{ kind: "rule-decl"; ruleName: string; params: Array<{ name: string; type: OpaqueExpr }>; returnType: OpaqueExpr; body: OpaqueExpr }` representing a synthesised top-level recursive rule declaration. Emission downstream (in `extract.ts` or the Pant emitter) renders this as a Pant rule with body — the existing `equation` variant assumes the rule is pre-declared with a separate signature.

Add a `if (stmt.kind === "while") { return lowerFixedPointL1BodyToSsaResult(stmt, options); }` arm before the existing `kind: "for"` dispatch. Routes to a thin adapter that calls `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body` from `ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` and wraps the result in the `IR1SsaBodyLowerResult` shape consistent with the scalar / collection / counter-loop arms.
requiredChanges.signature: function lowerFixedPointL1BodyToSsaResult(stmt: Extract<IR1Stmt, { kind: "while" }>, options: SsaBodyLowerOptions): IR1SsaBodyLowerResult

requiredChanges.description: When `buildL1LetWhileMutation` returns null (L3's bounded recognition failed), fall through to `buildL1LetWhileFixedPointMutation` instead of letting the let-rejection fire. This new helper constructs `ir1While(cond, mergedBody)` where mergedBody is the while-body with no trailing-step stripping (since there's no canonical counter step to strip). If the helper also returns null (e.g., the while shape doesn't fit fixed-point either — currently only if cond is literal-true), then fall through to the existing let rejection.
requiredChanges.signature: function buildL1LetWhileFixedPointMutation(letStmt: ts.VariableStatement, whileStmt: ts.WhileStatement, ctx: { ... }): IR1Stmt | { unsupported: string } | null

requiredChanges.description: Replace the WhileStatement catch-all rejection with `buildL1BareWhileMutation`. This new helper detects literal-true guards and rejects with the new diagnostic; otherwise constructs `ir1While(cond, body)` for the lower path. Literal-true detection uses `unwrapTransparentExpression` + a `kind: "BooleanLiteral"` check with `text === "true"`.
requiredChanges.signature: function buildL1BareWhileMutation(stmt: ts.WhileStatement, ctx: { ... }): IR1Stmt | { unsupported: string }

requiredChanges.description: Create fixture file with four exported functions: (a) `adjustBalanceTo(a, target, step)` — `while (a.balance < target) { a.balance += step; }` (single-location accumulator with non-counter variant); (b) `fillUpTo(a, cap)` — `while (a.size < cap) { a.size++; }` (incrementing toward target); (c) `drainTo(a, floor)` — `while (a.balance > floor) { a.balance -= 1; }` (decrementing toward floor); (d) `spin(a)` — `while (true) { a.tickCount++; }` (deliberate-reject literal-true fixture).

Snapshot update after the fixture lands. The implementing agent runs `just ts2pant-test-update-snapshots`. The diff should contain only the new fixture's emitted Pant (one synthesised recursive helper rule + primed-equation caller per passing function) plus the `> UNSUPPORTED:` line for the spin fixture, with no changes to existing fixture snapshots.

Add per-passing-fixture `pant --check` integration tests for `adjustBalanceTo`, `fillUpTo`, `drainTo`. Configure a 3000ms timeout. Add a `KNOWN_SLOW_CHECKS` constant listing any fixture whose `--check` exceeds the default timeout, with per-fixture rationale in JSDoc — per the L4 DoD's "documented SMT timeout" allowance.

Add a new subsection `## Fixed-point while loop lowering (L4)` immediately after `## Bounded while loop lowering (L3)`. Document the synthesised-recursive-rule shape, the build-path routes (let-then-while fall-through, bare while), the IR1SsaLoopBody discriminant (`terminationMetric: null`), the literal-true rejection, the multi-location rejection, the `define-fun-rec` Pant SMT emission, and the per-fixture timeout policy.

Update the General-loop SSA Workstream bullet under § Intermediate Representation pointer to reflect L4's landing. Add an entry under § Foundational Pattern noting that L4 realises the uninterpreted-predicate / Tarski-Knaster pattern as a recursive Pant rule lowered via `define-fun-rec`.

Add `**Status**: Landed in `ts2pant-fixed-point-while-lowering`. See `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md` section `## Fixed-point while loop lowering (L4)` ...` under Milestone 4. Update the Operator Actions block to reflect L4's landing (whether L5 break/continue proceeds, whether the recursive-rule output style passed external review).

functionalChanges.description: `lib/smt.ml` detects rules that syntactically reference themselves in their body and emits `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)` instead of the prior `(declare-fun NAME ...) + (assert (forall ((args …)) (= (NAME args) BODY)))` shape. Non-recursive rules continue to emit the existing shape unchanged. The two encodings are logically equivalent, but `define-fun-rec` gives Z3/CVC5's built-in induction tactics a hook the universally-quantified axiom does not.

`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` exports `recognizeFixedPointLoopShape` and `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body`. The recognizer accepts `kind: "while"` IR1Stmt with a single mutated location and a non-literal guard. The lowerer constructs an `IR1SsaLoopBody` with `terminationMetric: null` (the L1 fixed-point discriminant) and returns two propositions: a `kind: "rule-decl"` for the synthesised recursive helper, and a `kind: "equation"` for the primed-location caller.

`tools/ts2pant/src/types.ts` adds a `kind: "rule-decl"` variant to `PropResult` carrying `ruleName`, `params`, `returnType`, and `body`. Downstream emission renders this as a Pant rule declaration with body; the existing `equation` variant continues to represent post-state property equations.

`buildSupportedSsaMutatingBody` in `tools/ts2pant/src/translate-body.ts` routes two new TS while-loop surface shapes to fixed-point lowering: (a) `let counter = INIT; while (P) { body }` pairs that fail L3's bounded recognition fall through to L4 instead of triggering the let-rejection; (b) bare `while (P) { body }` without a preceding let constructs `ir1While(P, body)` for the lower path. Literal-true guards (`while (true)`) reject with `"while loop has no observable termination condition (literal-true guard); rewrite with a guard that depends on mutated state"`.

`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-lower-body.ts` dispatches `kind: "while"` IR1Stmt to the new fixed-point lowerer (via a thin adapter that wraps `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body`). Multi-location while-body modifications reject with `"fixed-point while lowering supports single-location bodies only; this loop modifies N rules"`. Combines cleanly with the existing scalar / collection / counter-loop arms.

Three passing fixed-point fixtures (`adjustBalanceTo`, `fillUpTo`, `drainTo`) translate end-to-end, pass `pant` typecheck, and either pass `pant --check` or surface a documented SMT timeout in `KNOWN_SLOW_CHECKS`. One deliberate-reject fixture (`spin`, `while (true)`) emits `> UNSUPPORTED:` with the literal-true diagnostic.

Documentation updated: `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md` gains a `## Fixed-point while loop lowering (L4)` subsection. `tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md` updates the General-loop SSA Workstream pointer and the Foundational Pattern section to note L4's recursive-rule realisation of the uninterpreted-predicate / Tarski-Knaster grounding. `workstreams/ts2pant-general-loop-ssa.md` marks Milestone 4 as landed.

contextResources.why: Authoritative source for L4's Definition of Done (3 passing fixtures, documented timeouts, divergent-loop reject), Operator Actions, and locked carry-forward decisions (location SSA, opaque versions, rule-oriented frames, single bounded/fixed-point family discriminated by `terminationMetric`). The Established Precedents section catalogues every L4-relevant encoding candidate (define-fun-rec, CHC, k-induction, BMC, Liquid Haskell refinement reflection) — this gameplan picks define-fun-rec; the others remain documented for future evolution.

The `## Bounded counter loop lowering (L2)` and `## Bounded while loop lowering (L3)` subsections are the prose template L4's doc subsection mirrors. The IR1 SSA contract section enumerates `IR1SsaLoopBody.terminationMetric` as the bounded↔fixed-point discriminant — L4's emission must produce `terminationMetric: null` for every loop it handles. The SSA Foundations subsection documents the location-SSA discipline carry-forward.

L3's let-then-while peephole at translate-body.ts:4341-4374 is the structural template L4's bare-while peephole + let-then-while fall-through follow. `buildL1LetWhileMutation` (line 4571 onward) shows the return-null contract on bounded-recognition failure that L4 hooks into. The L2 counter-loop module (`ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts`) is the architectural sibling that L4's new module mirrors — read `lowerCounterLoopL1Body` (line 118 onward) for the IR1SsaLoopBody construction discipline (opens loop-header, observes joinVersion in body, closes with back-edge). The L3 fixture file is the snapshot-comparison baseline.

`IR1SsaLoopBody.terminationMetric: IR1SsaTerminationMetric | null` is the bounded/fixed-point discriminant (line ~535). `ir1SsaOpenLoopHeader` / `ir1SsaCloseLoopHeader` are the two-pass Cytron protocol L4 inherits. `ir1SsaLoopBody` rejects open header joins at construction. The fixed-point lowerer (Patch 3) must satisfy these invariants — specifically: open one header join per mutated location (single location for L4), make body writes observe the joinVersion, close after the back-edge write version is known, and pass null as the terminationMetric to mark the body fixed-point. Patch 4 routes ir1While through this contract via the lower-body dispatch arm.

Pant's existing rule emission is at smt_preamble.ml:165 (`(declare-fun NAME (ARGS) RET)`) plus universally-quantified axioms emitted from rule bodies. Patch 2's recursive-rule detection lives in smt.ml as a sibling to the existing translate_expr / extract_preconditions / alpha_rename_binders helpers. The detector walks the rule's body expression via the existing AST walker pattern (binder-aware via Bindlib helpers in smt_expr.ml). Emission of `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)` mirrors the existing `(declare-fun ...)` indentation/spacing — see Printf.sprintf calls in smt_preamble.ml for the format conventions.

Pant SMT output is snapshot-tested; the new recursive-rule emission changes SMT text for any fixture whose rules are recursive. `samples/07-pantagruel.pant` exercises self-referential rule definitions (e.g., `infer e env = ... (infer ...)`) and its SMT snapshot will change. Patch 2 must regenerate affected snapshots and confirm the new SMT output verifies through Z3/CVC5 (no regression in `pant --check` results across the existing corpus).

`IR1SsaBodyLowerResult` and the adapter helpers (`scalarSsaBodyLowerResult`, `collectionSsaBodyLowerResult`, `loopSsaBodyLowerResult`, `ir1SsaBodyLowerSuccess`, `ir1SsaBodyLowerUnsupported`) define the contract Patch 3's new lowerer must produce. `combineIR1SsaBodyLowerResults` is the join used by the block-statement walk in `lowerL1BodyToSsaResult` — Patch 4's dispatch arm threads through this helper. `lowerCounterLoopL1Body` (in `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts`) is the closest architectural sibling: same module shape, same exported lowerer signature, same `IR1SsaBodyLowerResult` return type — Patch 3 mirrors its file layout.

The document-wide `NameRegistry` is the source of unique synthesised identifiers used across ts2pant (anonymous record domains, Map<K,V> synth sorts, μ-search comprehension binders, etc.). Patch 3's synthesised recursive-rule helper name follows the same discipline: kebab-cased base name (`fn--loop`), suffixed with collision counter (`fn--loop`, `fn--loop-1`, `fn--loop-2`, …) via `cellRegisterName`. Multiple fixed-point loops in the same source get distinct helper-rule identifiers without coordination.

Reynolds, Iosif, Tinelli et al.'s SMT-LIB 2.5+ standard — defines the `(define-fun-rec ...)` and `(define-funs-rec ...)` syntactic forms Patch 2 emits. The standard's section on recursive function definitions specifies argument sorts, return sort, and body shape. Z3's documentation page on recursive functions (https://microsoft.github.io/z3guide/docs/theories/Recursive-Functions/) confirms support and the built-in induction tactic invariants Patch 2 relies on.

Cousot & Cousot 1977 abstract interpretation — the semantic foundation Patch 3 implements and Patch 4 wires. The recursive Pant rule `loop-fn x = cond guard(x) => loop-fn (body x), true => x.` is the least fixed point of the operator `λf. λx. if guard(x) then f(body(x)) else x`. The Tarski–Knaster theorem (also cited at the workstream level) guarantees the lfp exists for the monotone operator. This is the why Patch 3's emission shape is correct and why Patch 4's build-path is justified in routing unbounded while to that shape; the implementing agent reads this to understand why the lowering preserves the loop's denotational semantics.

Plotkin 1975 CPS — the standard tail-recursive desugar `while P body ≡ let rec loop s = if P(s) then loop(body(s)) else s`. Patch 3's lowering is exactly this rewrite, with the location-SSA versions threaded as `s`. Cited in the workstream's Established Precedents for L4 specifically; the implementing agent reads this to confirm the bridge between `IR1Stmt.while` and the synthesised recursive Pant rule is mechanical (no novel transformation).

Vazou et al. POPL 2018 'Refinement Reflection: Complete Verification with SMT' — the closest existing system to L4's recursive-rule emission. Refinement reflection makes recursive function bodies visible to the SMT solver as logical relations rather than uninterpreted symbols, using `define-fun-rec` plus definitional axioms. The L4 emission (Patch 2 + Patch 3 together) is the Pant-translator instance of this technique. The paper's PLE / proof-by-logical-evaluation discussion informs the SMT-performance expectations for `pant --check` on L4 fixtures.

acceptanceCriteria: `lib/smt.ml` exports `is_rule_recursive` and emits `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)` for any rule whose body syntactically references itself; non-recursive rules emit the existing `(declare-fun ...) + (assert (forall ...))` shape unchanged.
`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` exists, mirroring `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts` in file layout, and exports `recognizeFixedPointLoopShape` + `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body`.
`tools/ts2pant/src/types.ts` adds a `rule-decl` variant to `PropResult` with the documented signature.

`tools/ts2pant/src/translate-body.ts` routes let-then-while pairs that fail L3 bounded recognition and bare while loops to `ir1While` construction; literal-true while loops reject with the new "no observable termination condition" diagnostic.
`tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-lower-body.ts` dispatches `kind: "while"` IR1Stmt through the new fixed-point lowerer.
Multi-location while bodies reject with the multi-location diagnostic.

Three passing fixtures (`adjustBalanceTo`, `fillUpTo`, `drainTo`) pass `pant` typecheck; each either passes `pant --check` within the 3000ms default timeout or appears in `KNOWN_SLOW_CHECKS` with documented per-fixture rationale.
The `spin` deliberate-reject fixture emits `> UNSUPPORTED:` with the literal-true diagnostic in the snapshot.
All existing ts2pant unit and integration tests pass; Pant snapshot tests pass after regenerating any snapshots affected by Patch 2's recursive-rule emission.
`tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md` documents the L4 lowering surface in a `## Fixed-point while loop lowering (L4)` subsection.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Emit recursive Pant rules as SMT-LIB `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)` rather than keeping the existing `(declare-fun NAME ...) + (assert (forall ((args …)) (= (NAME args) BODY)))` axiom shape.
explicitOpinions.rationale: The two encodings are logically equivalent, but `define-fun-rec` gives Z3 and CVC5 native induction-tactic hooks the universally-quantified axiom cannot trigger. The workstream's L4 DoD allows documented `pant --check` timeouts but encouraged minimising them; `define-fun-rec` is the cheapest path to better SMT automation. The Pant runtime change is small and self-contained (one detection helper + one branch in the rule emitter); existing non-recursive rules are unaffected. Liquid Haskell refinement reflection (Vazou et al. POPL 2018, cited in the workstream precedents) validates this is the right encoding for the closest peer system.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Always emit the recursive rule directly. Do not BMC-unroll N iterations into a `cond` chain before falling back to recursion.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Single canonical lowering shape: every unbounded while produces one recursive rule definition. Simpler implementation, simpler review, simpler debugging. The SMT-performance vs spec-readability trade-off the workstream OQ identifies has been on SV-COMP's leaderboard for twenty years; getting the unroll-depth right per fixture would be a separate optimisation effort with its own gameplan. L4's DoD explicitly allows documented timeouts as an acceptable acceptance criterion, which gives the recursive-only shape room to ship without solver-tuning.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Synthesise a named top-level recursive helper rule (`fn--loop-N` pattern) rather than attempting an anonymous-recursive inline form.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Pantagruel has no anonymous-recursive expression syntax — the rule must be named to be recursive at all. The only real design choice is the naming convention. `fn--loop` (enclosing function name + double-dash + `loop`) follows the existing `account--balance` qualifier discipline (M5 property-access lowering) and the `NameRegistry`'s collision-suffixing produces unique identifiers for multiple loops in one source. The recursive rule is a peer of the function's primed equations — it stands alongside, not inside.

explicitOpinions.opinion: L4 accepts two TS surface routes into fixed-point lowering: (a) `let counter = INIT; while (P) { body }` pairs that fail L3 bounded recognition fall through to L4; (b) bare `while (P) { body }` without a preceding `let` routes directly to L4. `do-while` and labelled loops remain unsupported.
explicitOpinions.rationale: These two routes cover the realistic shapes: unbounded counter searches (where L3's bound extraction failed because the bound is dynamic, e.g., `s.has(i)`) and predicate-driven loops (`while (a.balance < target)`). `do-while` is rare enough that it can be a follow-on; labelled loops are a more substantial change (break/continue targeting). Limiting scope keeps Patch 4 reviewable; the workstream's L5 milestone is the natural home for break/continue + do-while extensions.

explicitOpinions.opinion: L4 supports single-location loop bodies only. Multi-location bodies (the body modifies more than one rule) reject with a targeted diagnostic.
explicitOpinions.rationale: A multi-location loop would need the recursive helper's return type to be a tuple or synthesised record domain, with destructuring in each modified rule's primed equation. The anonymous-record-synth machinery (M5) could in principle synthesise the return-type domain, but the lowering and pant --check tractability questions multiply (more solver state per iteration, more interaction with refinement reflection). Limiting to single-location is the same constraint L2 already adopts for counter loops; preserves symmetry, keeps the recursive-rule signature scalar. Multi-location is a clear follow-on candidate.

explicitOpinions.opinion: `while (true)` literal-true loops reject at the build pass with a specific "no observable termination condition" diagnostic, not at lowering time.
explicitOpinions.rationale: A literal-true guard makes the recursive rule a divergent definition (no fixed point exists). Pant would type-check it and `pant --check` would either return unsat or time out — neither is useful debugging signal. Detecting at the build pass turns a potential silent SMT pathology into an actionable error message. The detection is mechanical (L1 expression inspection for `lit { kind: bool, value: true }` after const-folding). The DoD's "failure-mode test confirms genuinely divergent loops surface a clear diagnostic" is satisfied by this build-time reject.

explicitOpinions.opinion: The fixed-point lowerer lives in a new module `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts`, parallel to `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts`. The `ir1-lower-body.ts` dispatch adds a `kind: "while"` arm that wraps the new helper.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Mirrors the L2 split. Keeps fixed-point lowering's classifier + emitter logic in one file (the recognizer, the synthesised-rule constructor, the IR1SsaProgram assembly). The architectural twin to `lowerCounterLoopL1Body` is `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body` — same signature shape, same return type. A future L6 (summary unification) that wants to merge bounded and fixed-point paths has two clearly-named modules to consolidate.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Extend `PropResult` with a new `kind: "rule-decl"` variant for the synthesised recursive helper. Reuse the existing `kind: "equation"` for the primed-location caller.
explicitOpinions.rationale: The recursive helper is not an equation in the existing sense (which is a primed-property post-state) — it is a fresh rule declaration with its own signature and body. Adding a new variant is cleaner than overloading `equation`. The downstream emitter (`extract.ts` / Pant emitter) gets one new dispatch arm; the existing equation emission is unchanged. This is also the natural home for future synthesised rules from L5 / L6 if they need to add helper definitions.

patches.title: Pending tests for L4 fixed-point lowering and define-fun-rec emission

files.description: New unit test file with `it.skip` stubs for the fixed-point lowerer's behaviors. Each stub carries a `// PENDING Patch <N>:` comment naming the implementing patch.
Add `it.skip` stubs for the build path's fixed-point routes: let-then-while fall-through builds `ir1While`; bare while builds `ir1While`; literal-true while rejects.
Add pending OCaml unit tests (tagged `[@tags "pending"]`) for `Smt.is_rule_recursive` and the recursive-rule emission shape.

changes: Create `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.test.mts` modeled on `tests/ir1-ssa-counter-loop.test.mts`'s shape (describe block, direct constructor calls, `node:assert/strict`).

Add skipped stubs: `it.skip("recognizes a single-location while-loop fixed-point shape", ...)`, `it.skip("rejects literal-true guards at the recognizer level", ...)`, `it.skip("rejects multi-location while bodies", ...)`, `it.skip("constructs IR1SsaLoopBody with terminationMetric: null", ...)`, `it.skip("synthesises a uniquely-named recursive helper rule via NameRegistry", ...)`, `it.skip("emits a rule-decl PropResult for the helper and an equation PropResult for the caller", ...)`.

In `tools/ts2pant/tests/translate-body.test.mts`, add skipped stubs: `it.skip("desugars let-then-while pair that fails L3 bounded recognition to ir1While", ...)`, `it.skip("builds ir1While for a bare while loop with non-literal guard", ...)`, `it.skip("rejects while (true) with no-observable-termination diagnostic", ...)`.

In `test/test_smt.ml`, add pending OCaml tests: that `Smt.is_rule_recursive` returns true on a declaration whose body's RHS applies the rule's own name; that the recursive emitter produces a `(define-fun-rec ...)` string with the rule's params/return-sort/body and that the corresponding `(declare-fun ...)` line is suppressed; that non-recursive rules continue to emit the existing shape.
Each stub body contains only the PENDING comment naming the implementing patch — no constructor calls or assertions that reference Patch-2/3/4 symbols (those symbols / shapes don't exist yet).
Do not modify any non-test file in this patch.
testStubsIntroduced: ir1-ssa-fixed-point > recognizes a single-location while-loop fixed-point shape

ir1-ssa-fixed-point > constructs IR1SsaLoopBody with terminationMetric: null
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > synthesises a uniquely-named recursive helper rule via NameRegistry
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > emits a rule-decl PropResult for the helper and an equation PropResult for the caller

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_FIXED_POINT_WHILE_LOWERING_PATCH_1.

> Patch 1 lands the test scaffolding for L4. Stubs are skipped;
> Patches 2 / 3 / 4 unskip them in-place with concrete
> assertions. No runtime invariants change.

TestStub.
PendingFor.
patch-2 => PendingFor.
patch-3 => PendingFor.
patch-4 => PendingFor.

stub-pending-for s: TestStub => PendingFor.
---
true.

patches.title: Pant: emit define-fun-rec for self-referential rule definitions

files.description: Add `is_rule_recursive : Ast.declaration -> bool` that walks a rule's body expression and returns true when the body contains an application whose head matches the rule's own name. Add a sibling emitter for the recursive case that produces `(define-fun-rec NAME ((p1 S1) (p2 S2) …) RET-SORT BODY)`. Dispatch the existing rule-emission path on `is_rule_recursive`.

Update the rule-declaration emission at line 165 to suppress the `(declare-fun NAME …)` line when `Smt.is_rule_recursive` returns true on the rule. The `(define-fun-rec …)` line emitted by smt.ml replaces it.

Remove `[@tags "pending"]` from the Patch-2-owned stubs in Patch 1. Replace each PENDING-comment body with concrete assertions: build a small `Ast.declaration` for a self-referential rule, call `Smt.is_rule_recursive`, assert true; check emitted SMT text contains `define-fun-rec` and does not contain `declare-fun NAME`. Build a non-recursive rule, assert false + the unchanged shape.

Regenerate any SMT-text snapshots affected by the new emission. Fixtures whose rules are recursive (e.g., `samples/07-pantagruel.pant` exercising `infer e env = ... (infer ...)`) will see their SMT text change to the new `define-fun-rec` shape. Confirm `dune test` passes after regeneration.

changes: In `lib/smt.ml`, add `is_rule_recursive (decl : Ast.declaration) : bool`. Walk the body expression via the existing AST traversal (mutually recursive with `translate_expr` style). A rule's name comes from `decl.d_head` (Lower identifier); a recursive reference is an `EApp` whose head is `EVar (Lower decl_name)`. Use `Smt_expr.fold_expr` (or the equivalent existing walker) — do NOT hand-roll a new traversal; reuse Bindlib-backed primitives so binders are scope-correct.

In `lib/smt.ml`, add `emit_rule_recursive (env : Env.t) (decl : Ast.declaration) : string`. Returns one SMT line: `(define-fun-rec NAME ((p1 S1) (p2 S2) …) RET-SORT BODY-EXPR)`. Parameter sorts come from `decl.d_params` via the existing `Smt_types.sort_of_type`; return sort comes from `decl.d_return_type`; body expression comes from `decl.d_body` via `translate_expr config env`. No surrounding `(assert ...)` or `(forall ...)` — `define-fun-rec` implicitly quantifies over the formal params.

In `lib/smt_preamble.ml:165`, where the existing `(declare-fun %s (%s) %s)\n` line is emitted per rule, branch on `Smt.is_rule_recursive` (passing the rule's declaration). When recursive: skip the `(declare-fun ...)` line entirely (define-fun-rec emitted later from the body-emission pass subsumes it). When non-recursive: keep the existing behavior unchanged. The primed-rule `(declare-fun NAME_prime …)` line stays — primed rules represent next-state and are never recursive themselves (they reference but do not call the primed rule).

Verify the body-emission pass that currently produces `(assert (forall ((args …)) (= (NAME args) BODY)))` for each rule body becomes `(define-fun-rec NAME (ARGS) RET BODY)` via `emit_rule_recursive` for recursive rules. The body translation logic (translate_expr) is unchanged — only the wrapping changes.

Run `dune test` to surface any snapshot test failures. For each failure, inspect the diff: if the change is the expected `declare-fun + forall` → `define-fun-rec` rewrite, regenerate the snapshot. If any other change appears, halt and debug — that's a regression.
Unskip the three Patch-2-owned `test/test_smt.ml` stubs. Replace each PENDING comment with concrete assertions matching the existing test patterns in `test_smt.ml`.
Do NOT modify any ts2pant file in this patch. Patch 2 is Pant-side only.

precedents.name: SMT-LIB 2.6 — define-fun-rec
precedents.whyApplicable: Section on recursive function definitions specifies the `(define-fun-rec NAME ((p1 S1) … (pn Sn)) RET BODY)` syntactic form Patch 2 emits. Argument sorts match the rule's parameter types; return sort matches the rule's return type; body is the rule's RHS expression. The standard also defines `(define-funs-rec ...)` for mutual recursion — out of scope for L4 (single-loop functions only) but worth knowing about for L5/L6.

precedents.name: Vazou et al. POPL 2018 — Refinement Reflection
precedents.whyApplicable: Closest peer system for what Patch 2 enables: making recursive function bodies SMT-visible via `define-fun-rec` and definitional axioms. The paper's PLE / proof-by-logical-evaluation discussion informs the SMT-performance expectations — Z3's built-in induction tactic handles many shapes automatically but bounded unfolding remains a useful fallback (relevant to per-fixture timeout policy in Patch 4).

testStubsImplemented: smt > is_rule_recursive detects self-referential rule body
smt > recursive rule emits define-fun-rec form with body and skips declare-fun line
smt > non-recursive rule emits declare-fun + universal-axiom unchanged
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_FIXED_POINT_WHILE_LOWERING_PATCH_2.

> Patch 2 extends Pant's SMT backend to detect self-referential
> rule definitions and emit them as SMT-LIB define-fun-rec
> instead of declare-fun + universal axiom. The two encodings
> are logically equivalent (both define the recursive function
> by its equation); define-fun-rec gives Z3 / CVC5 native
> induction hooks the universal axiom does not.

Declaration.
Rule.
Expr.
SmtLine.
EmissionShape.
decl-fun-rec => EmissionShape.
decl-fun-plus-axiom => EmissionShape.

is-recursive? r: Rule => Bool.
emission-shape r: Rule => EmissionShape.
body-of r: Rule => Expr.
body-applies-self? r: Rule, e: Expr => Bool.
emits-define-fun-rec? r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] => Bool.
emits-declare-fun? r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] => Bool.
emits-universal-axiom? r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] => Bool.
logically-equivalent? a: EmissionShape, b: EmissionShape => Bool.
---

> A rule is recursive iff its body applies its own name.
all r: Rule | is-recursive? r <-> body-applies-self? r (body-of r).

> Recursive rules emit define-fun-rec and skip declare-fun.
all r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] |
  is-recursive? r ->
    emits-define-fun-rec? r lines and ~(emits-declare-fun? r lines).

> Non-recursive rules emit declare-fun + universal axiom unchanged.
all r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] |
  ~(is-recursive? r) ->
    emits-declare-fun? r lines and emits-universal-axiom? r lines.

> The two emission shapes are logically equivalent.
logically-equivalent? decl-fun-rec decl-fun-plus-axiom.

patches.title: ts2pant: fixed-point loop lowerer module

files.description: New module exporting `recognizeFixedPointLoopShape` and `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body`. Mirrors `ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts` in file layout. Constructs IR1SsaLoopBody with terminationMetric: null; synthesises a top-level recursive helper rule via the NameRegistry; emits a rule-decl PropResult for the helper and an equation PropResult for the caller.
Extend `PropResult` with a `kind: "rule-decl"` variant carrying `ruleName: string`, `params: Array<{ name: string; type: OpaqueExpr }>`, `returnType: OpaqueExpr`, `body: OpaqueExpr`.

Add a dispatch arm for `kind: "rule-decl"` in the PropResult emission pass. Renders the rule-decl as a Pant rule declaration with body (parameter list, return-type arrow, body expression). Sits alongside the existing equation and assertion arms.
Remove `.skip` from every stub introduced in Patch 1. Replace each PENDING-comment body with concrete assertions.

changes: Create `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.ts` with the module shape mirroring `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-ssa-counter-loop.ts`.

Implement `recognizeFixedPointLoopShape(stmt: Extract<IR1Stmt, { kind: 'while' }>)`. Walk the body to collect mutated locations (using the same property-mutation detection logic the counter-loop module uses). Reject with `{ unsupported: 'fixed-point while lowering supports single-location bodies only; this loop modifies N rules' }` if there is more than one. Reject with `{ unsupported: 'while loop has a literal-true guard; cannot define a fixed point' }` if `stmt.cond.kind === 'lit' && stmt.cond.value.kind === 'bool' && stmt.cond.value.value === true`. On success, return `{ guardExpr: stmt.cond, bodyStmt: stmt.body, mutatedLocation: <the single location> }`.

Implement `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body(stmt, options)`. Open an `IR1SsaLoopHeaderJoin` for the mutated location via `ir1SsaOpenLoopHeader`. Build the body's effect-on-location expression: walk the body, find the single property write, build an IR1Expr representing `body(s)` where `s` is the current location value. Construct an `IR1SsaWrite` whose value is that effect expression. Close the header via `ir1SsaCloseLoopHeader` with the write's version. Construct `IR1SsaLoopBody` with `terminationMetric: null` (the L1 fixed-point discriminant). Allocate a synthesised helper rule name via `cellRegisterName(synthCell, 'fn--loop')` — the document-wide registry produces a unique kebab-cased name.

Emit two PropResults: (a) `{ kind: 'rule-decl', ruleName, params: [{ name: 's', type: <location-type> }, ...loop-invariant-args], returnType: <location-type>, body: cond(guard, helperCall(body-effect), s) }`; (b) `{ kind: 'equation', lhs: <primed-location>, rhs: helperCall(<initial-location-value>, ...loop-invariant-args), quantifiers: [] }`. The helper call uses `ast.app(ast.var(ruleName), [args])`; the cond uses `ast.cond([[guard, helperCall(body-effect)], [ast.litBool(true), ast.var('s')]])`.

Extend `tools/ts2pant/src/types.ts` `PropResult` union with the new `rule-decl` variant. Update consumers (`extract.ts`, any pretty-printer) to handle the new variant — emit it as a Pant rule declaration followed by its body equation (one rule, two lines: signature + body).

Identify loop-invariant arguments by walking the body and finding any free variables that aren't the mutated location or its receiver. These become extra params to the helper (e.g., `target`, `step`, `cap` in the L4 fixtures). The helper's signature is `fn--loop s: T, target: T, step: T, … => T.`

Unskip the six Patch-3-owned stubs in `tools/ts2pant/tests/ir1-ssa-fixed-point.test.mts` and replace each PENDING-comment body with concrete assertions exercising the recognizer + emission shape on hand-constructed `ir1While` IR1Stmts.
No caller in this patch constructs a `kind: 'while'` IR1Stmt that reaches this module — the build path's wiring lands in Patch 4. Patch 3 ships dormant capability.

precedents.name: Cytron 1991 SSA construction (carry-forward)
precedents.whyApplicable: The two-pass loop-header phi-placement protocol from L1 is unchanged in L4: `ir1SsaOpenLoopHeader` then body emission then `ir1SsaCloseLoopHeader`. The fixed-point lowerer's only difference from the bounded counter-loop lowerer is that `terminationMetric: null` marks the body fixed-point; the SSA construction discipline (single header join per mutated location, body reads observe joinVersion, back-edge write closes the header) is identical.

precedents.name: Cousot & Cousot 1977 — Abstract Interpretation / fixed-point loop semantics
precedents.whyApplicable: The semantic foundation: a while loop's post-state is the least fixed point of the body's iteration. The recursive Pant rule `fn--loop s = cond guard(s) => fn--loop (body s), true => s.` is exactly this lfp encoded as a function. The Tarski–Knaster theorem guarantees the lfp exists for the monotone iteration operator.

precedents.name: Plotkin 1975 — CPS / tail-recursive desugar of while
precedents.whyApplicable: The mechanical bridge: `while P body ≡ let rec loop s = if P(s) then loop(body(s)) else s`. Patch 3's emission is exactly this rewrite. The location-SSA version (Cytron) provides `s`; the guard and body come from the IR1Stmt; the helper-call site is the back-edge of the SSA loop-header join.

testStubsImplemented: ir1-ssa-fixed-point > recognizes a single-location while-loop fixed-point shape
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > rejects literal-true guards at the recognizer level
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > constructs IR1SsaLoopBody with terminationMetric: null
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > synthesises a uniquely-named recursive helper rule via NameRegistry
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > emits a rule-decl PropResult for the helper and an equation PropResult for the caller
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_FIXED_POINT_WHILE_LOWERING_PATCH_3.

> Patch 3 lands the fixed-point lowerer module. The recognizer
> classifies single-location while bodies; the emitter
> constructs an IR1SsaLoopBody with terminationMetric: null
> (the L1 fixed-point discriminant) and produces a synthesised
> top-level recursive helper rule plus a primed-equation caller.
> No caller in this patch reaches the new code; capability is
> dormant until Patch 4 wires the build path.

IR1Stmt.
IR1SsaLoopBody.
IR1SsaLocation.
PropResult.
RecognizerResult.
WhileStmt.
RuleName.
TerminationMetric.

recognize-result s: WhileStmt => RecognizerResult.
is-single-location? r: RecognizerResult => Bool.
is-literal-true? s: WhileStmt => Bool.
mutated-count s: WhileStmt => Nat0.
lower-result s: WhileStmt => [PropResult].
body-metric b: IR1SsaLoopBody => TerminationMetric.
null-metric? m: TerminationMetric => Bool.
emits-rule-decl? results: [PropResult] => Bool.
emits-equation? results: [PropResult] => Bool.
helper-name-unique? n: RuleName => Bool.
---

> Literal-true while statements reject at the recognizer.
all s: WhileStmt | is-literal-true? s -> ~(is-single-location? (recognize-result s)).

> Multi-location while statements reject at the recognizer.
all s: WhileStmt | mutated-count s > 1 -> ~(is-single-location? (recognize-result s)).

> Successfully-recognized while statements yield a fixed-point loop body.
all s: WhileStmt, b: IR1SsaLoopBody |
  is-single-location? (recognize-result s) ->
    null-metric? (body-metric b).

> The lowered PropResult list contains both a rule-decl and an equation.
all s: WhileStmt |
  is-single-location? (recognize-result s) ->
    emits-rule-decl? (lower-result s) and emits-equation? (lower-result s).

> The synthesised helper rule name is unique within the document.
all s: WhileStmt, n: RuleName |
  is-single-location? (recognize-result s) ->
    helper-name-unique? n.

patches.title: ts2pant: wire build path + lower path; ship fixtures + integration tests

files.description: Add `buildL1LetWhileFixedPointMutation` for the let-then-while fall-through route. Add `buildL1BareWhileMutation` for the bare-while route with literal-true detection. Modify the existing peephole at line 4374 to call the fixed-point helper when `buildL1LetWhileMutation` returns null. Modify the WhileStatement reject at line 4493 to call `buildL1BareWhileMutation`.
Add `lowerFixedPointL1BodyToSsaResult` adapter and dispatch `kind: "while"` IR1Stmt through it (sits alongside the existing kind: "for" arm).
Three passing fixtures (asc accumulator, asc counter, dec counter) + one literal-true reject.
Snapshot update after the fixture lands. Run `just ts2pant-test-update-snapshots`.
Per-fixture `pant --check` integration tests with a 3000ms default timeout. `KNOWN_SLOW_CHECKS` constant lists any fixture exceeding the default, with per-fixture rationale.
Unskip the three Patch-4-owned stubs from Patch 1 and replace each PENDING-comment body with concrete assertions.

changes: Implement `buildL1LetWhileFixedPointMutation(letStmt, whileStmt, ctx)` in `tools/ts2pant/src/translate-body.ts`. Build the let's init as L1, build the while's condition as L1, build the while's body as a Block of L1 statements (no trailing-step stripping — the body's mutations are what the fixed-point lowerer will analyse). Construct `ir1Block([ir1Let(counterName, initExpr), ir1While(condExpr, bodyStmt)])`. Returns `IR1Stmt | { unsupported: string } | null` — null on shape mismatch (the let isn't a simple binding, the while's body doesn't translate, etc.) to let the existing rejection fire.

Modify the peephole at translate-body.ts:4374. When `buildL1LetWhileMutation` returns null, before falling through to the existing branches, call `buildL1LetWhileFixedPointMutation(stmt, whileStmt, ctx)`. On non-null IR1Stmt: push to stmts, increment i, continue. On null: fall through to the existing branches (the let rejection fires as today). On `unsupported`: return that as the overall body's rejection.

Implement `buildL1BareWhileMutation(stmt, ctx)` in `tools/ts2pant/src/translate-body.ts`. Unwrap transparent expressions from `stmt.expression`. If the unwrapped expression is `ts.SyntaxKind.TrueKeyword` (literal true), return `{ unsupported: 'while loop has no observable termination condition (literal-true guard); rewrite with a guard that depends on mutated state' }`. Otherwise build the guard as L1, build the body as L1, return `ir1While(guardExpr, bodyStmt)`.

Modify the WhileStatement catch-all at translate-body.ts:4493. Replace the unconditional rejection with `buildL1BareWhileMutation(stmt, ctx)`. The new helper handles both the literal-true reject and the successful L4 route.

Add `lowerFixedPointL1BodyToSsaResult(stmt, options)` adapter in `tools/ts2pant/src/ir1-lower-body.ts`. Calls `lowerFixedPointLoopL1Body` from Patch 3's module, wraps the result in the `IR1SsaBodyLowerResult` shape consistent with the scalar/collection/counter-loop arms. Catches errors and converts them to `ir1SsaBodyLowerUnsupported`.

Add the dispatch arm in `lowerL1BodyToSsaResult` at line ~114: `if (stmt.kind === 'while') { return lowerFixedPointL1BodyToSsaResult(stmt, options); }` — placed before the existing `kind: 'for'` arm. The existing arms are unchanged.

Create `tools/ts2pant/tests/fixtures/constructs/functions-mutating-fixed-point-while.ts` with four exported functions: `adjustBalanceTo(a: Account, target: number, step: number): void` (`while (a.balance < target) { a.balance += step; }`), `fillUpTo(a: Account, cap: number): void` (`while (a.size < cap) { a.size++; }`), `drainTo(a: Account, floor: number): void` (`while (a.balance > floor) { a.balance -= 1; }`), and `spin(a: Account): void` (`while (true) { a.tickCount++; }` — deliberate-reject fixture). The `Account` interface uses fields appropriate for each fixture (balance, size, tickCount) — keep the interface compact.

Run `just ts2pant-test-update-snapshots` and inspect `tools/ts2pant/tests/constructs.test.mts.snapshot` for the four new fixture entries: three passing fixtures each producing a `fn--loop` recursive helper rule + a primed-equation caller; the spin fixture producing `> UNSUPPORTED:` with the literal-true diagnostic.

Add per-passing-fixture `pant --check` integration tests in `tools/ts2pant/tests/integration/e2e.test.mts`. Use the existing `runCheck` helper. Default timeout 3000ms. Add `const KNOWN_SLOW_CHECKS: Set<string> = new Set([...])` and add any fixture whose `--check` exceeds the default (likely none for the chosen single-location fixtures, but include the constant so future fixtures have a clear extension point).
Unskip the three Patch-4-owned `tools/ts2pant/tests/translate-body.test.mts` stubs from Patch 1 and replace each PENDING-comment body with concrete assertions.
Run `just ts2pant-test` and `just ts2pant-test-integration` to confirm all existing tests pass and the new ones do too.

precedents.name: Cytron 1991 SSA construction (carry-forward)
precedents.whyApplicable: The build path's `ir1While` construction is the L1 form that flows into Patch 3's SSA framework. The two-pass loop-header join discipline is unchanged from L1/L2/L3 — Patch 4's wiring just routes the right TS surface shapes to the right L1 form.

testStubsImplemented: translate-body > desugars let-then-while pair that fails L3 bounded recognition to ir1While
translate-body > builds ir1While for a bare while loop with non-literal guard
translate-body > rejects while (true) with no-observable-termination diagnostic
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_FIXED_POINT_WHILE_LOWERING_PATCH_4.

> Patch 4 activates the L4 pipeline. The build path constructs
> ir1While for two routes: let-then-while pairs that fail L3,
> and bare while loops. The lower path dispatches kind: while
> to the fixed-point lowerer from Patch 3. Three passing
> fixtures ship plus one deliberate-reject literal-true fixture.

TSStmt.
IR1Stmt.
BuildOutcome.
LowerResult.
FixtureKind.
passing => FixtureKind.
literal-true-reject => FixtureKind.

build-mutating-body s: TSStmt => BuildOutcome.
is-ir1-while? b: BuildOutcome => Bool.
is-rejection? b: BuildOutcome => Bool.
rejection-mentions-literal-true? b: BuildOutcome => Bool.
rejection-mentions-multi-location? r: LowerResult => Bool.
is-bare-while? s: TSStmt => Bool.
is-let-then-while-pair? s: TSStmt => Bool.
l3-bounded-recognized? s: TSStmt => Bool.
lower-result s: IR1Stmt => LowerResult.
emits-recursive-helper? r: LowerResult => Bool.
emits-caller-equation? r: LowerResult => Bool.
---

> Bare non-literal-true while statements build to ir1While.
all s: TSStmt |
  is-bare-while? s and ~(rejection-mentions-literal-true? (build-mutating-body s)) ->
    is-ir1-while? (build-mutating-body s).

> Literal-true while statements reject with the no-observable-termination diagnostic.
all s: TSStmt |
  is-bare-while? s ->
    (is-rejection? (build-mutating-body s) ->
      rejection-mentions-literal-true? (build-mutating-body s)).

> Let-then-while pairs that fail L3 fall through to ir1While.
all s: TSStmt |
  is-let-then-while-pair? s and ~(l3-bounded-recognized? s) ->
    is-ir1-while? (build-mutating-body s).

> Successfully-built ir1While statements lower to a recursive helper + caller equation.
all stmt: IR1Stmt |
  emits-recursive-helper? (lower-result stmt) and
  emits-caller-equation? (lower-result stmt).

patches.title: Document L4 lowering surface; mark milestone landed

files.description: Add `## Fixed-point while loop lowering (L4)` subsection immediately after `## Bounded while loop lowering (L3)`. Document the synthesised-recursive-rule shape, the two build-path routes (let-then-while fall-through, bare while), the IR1SsaLoopBody discriminant (terminationMetric: null), the literal-true rejection, the multi-location rejection, the define-fun-rec Pant SMT emission, and the per-fixture timeout policy.

Update the General-loop SSA Workstream bullet under § Intermediate Representation pointer to note L4 landed. Add a brief reference under § Foundational Pattern noting L4 realises the Tarski-Knaster uninterpreted-predicate pattern via a recursive Pant rule emitted as define-fun-rec.

Add `**Status**: Landed in `ts2pant-fixed-point-while-lowering`. See `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md` section `## Fixed-point while loop lowering (L4)` for the recursive-rule lowering shape, the two build-path routes, and the documented per-fixture timeout policy.` under Milestone 4. Address the Operator Actions block — note that the recursive-rule output style should be validated by an external Pant reviewer post-merge.

changes: In `tools/ts2pant/docs/intermediate-representation.md`, add the new L4 subsection following the L2/L3 structure: surface forms (let-then-while fall-through + bare while), recognizer constraints (single mutated location, non-literal-true guard), synthesised helper rule shape, IR1SsaLoopBody discriminant, accumulator-fold / counter / decrement lowering examples, rejection diagnostics, Pant SMT emission (define-fun-rec), per-fixture timeout policy.

In `tools/ts2pant/AGENTS.md`, update the General-loop SSA Workstream bullet (currently lists L1/L2/L3 as landed) to add L4 to the landed list. Under § Foundational Pattern: Uninterpreted Predicates and Implicit Definition, add a brief reference noting L4 realises the pattern (the recursive helper rule is the implicitly-defined predicate; the loop-body iteration is the constraint system; the SMT solver via define-fun-rec computes the fixpoint).

In `workstreams/ts2pant-general-loop-ssa.md`, add the landed-status line under Milestone 4. Update the Operator Actions block (`Validate the recursive-rule output style with at least one external reviewer familiar with Pant`) to note it's still required post-merge — this is an out-of-band action, not gated by the gameplan.
Do not modify any source file in this patch.
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_FIXED_POINT_WHILE_LOWERING_PATCH_5.

> Patch 5 lands documentation. AGENTS.md gains the L4 landed
> status under General-loop SSA Workstream; the IR doc gains
> a L4 subsection; the workstream marks Milestone 4 as landed.
> No runtime invariants change.

Document.
DocumentKind.
MilestoneStatus.
agents-md => DocumentKind.
workstream-doc => DocumentKind.
ir-doc => DocumentKind.
pending => MilestoneStatus.
landed => MilestoneStatus.

document-kind d: Document => DocumentKind.
document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d: Document => Bool.
workstream-milestone-4-status => MilestoneStatus.
---

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = agents-md ->
    document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = ir-doc ->
    document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = workstream-doc ->
    document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d.

workstream-milestone-4-status = landed.

testMap.testName: smt > is_rule_recursive detects self-referential rule body
smt > recursive rule emits define-fun-rec form with body and skips declare-fun line
smt > non-recursive rule emits declare-fun + universal-axiom unchanged
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > recognizes a single-location while-loop fixed-point shape
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > rejects literal-true guards at the recognizer level
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > constructs IR1SsaLoopBody with terminationMetric: null
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > synthesises a uniquely-named recursive helper rule via NameRegistry
ir1-ssa-fixed-point > emits a rule-decl PropResult for the helper and an equation PropResult for the caller
translate-body > desugars let-then-while pair that fails L3 bounded recognition to ir1While
translate-body > builds ir1While for a bare while loop with non-literal guard
translate-body > rejects while (true) with no-observable-termination diagnostic

mergabilityInsight: 3 of 5 patches are INFRA. Patch 1 lands skipped test stubs. Patch 2 (BEHAVIOR) changes Pant's SMT emission for self-referential rules to define-fun-rec — minimal blast radius, affects only recursive rules. Patch 3 (INFRA) ships the dormant ts2pant fixed-point lowerer module. Patch 4 (BEHAVIOR) is the single ts2pant behavior-change patch — wires both runtimes together, ships fixtures, ships integration tests. Patch 5 documents. Patches 2 and 3 are parallelisable (different runtimes); Patch 4 waits for both.
finalStateSpec:
module TS2PANT_FIXED_POINT_WHILE_LOWERING.

> ════════════════════════════════
> Milestone 4 of the ts2pant general-loop SSA workstream.
> ════════════════════════════════
> After this milestone, the mutating-body translator accepts
> unbounded while loops (single mutated location, non-literal-
> true guard) and lowers them to a synthesised top-level
> recursive Pant rule. Pant's SMT backend emits the recursive
> rule as define-fun-rec, giving Z3 / CVC5 native induction
> hooks for pant --check. Literal-true and multi-location
> while bodies reject with targeted diagnostics. Three passing
> fixtures verify end-to-end through pant typecheck and
> pant --check; one deliberate-reject fixture documents the
> literal-true rejection. Documentation in AGENTS.md, the IR
> doc, and the workstream doc records the surface and marks
> the milestone landed.

TSStmt.
IR1Stmt.
WhileStmt.
Declaration.
Rule.
PropResult.
IR1SsaLoopBody.
IR1SsaLocation.
TerminationMetric.
BuildOutcome.
LowerResult.
SmtLine.
EmissionShape.
Document.
DocumentKind.
MilestoneStatus.
decl-fun-rec => EmissionShape.
decl-fun-plus-axiom => EmissionShape.
agents-md => DocumentKind.
ir-doc => DocumentKind.
workstream-doc => DocumentKind.
pending => MilestoneStatus.
landed => MilestoneStatus.

> Pant SMT emission (Patch 2).
is-recursive? r: Rule => Bool.
body-of r: Rule => IR1Stmt.
body-applies-self? r: Rule, e: IR1Stmt => Bool.
emission-shape r: Rule => EmissionShape.
emits-define-fun-rec? r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] => Bool.
emits-declare-fun? r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] => Bool.
emits-universal-axiom? r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] => Bool.
logically-equivalent? a: EmissionShape, b: EmissionShape => Bool.

> ts2pant fixed-point lowering (Patches 3, 4).
is-literal-true? s: WhileStmt => Bool.
mutated-count s: WhileStmt => Nat0.
build-mutating-body s: TSStmt => BuildOutcome.
lower-result-of-while s: WhileStmt => LowerResult.
is-ir1-while? b: BuildOutcome => Bool.
is-rejection? b: BuildOutcome => Bool.
rejection-mentions-literal-true? b: BuildOutcome => Bool.
recognizer-rejects-literal-true? s: WhileStmt => Bool.
recognizer-rejects-multi-location? s: WhileStmt => Bool.
is-bare-while? s: TSStmt => Bool.
is-let-then-while-pair? s: TSStmt => Bool.
l3-bounded-recognized? s: TSStmt => Bool.
body-metric b: IR1SsaLoopBody => TerminationMetric.
null-metric? m: TerminationMetric => Bool.
emits-recursive-helper? r: LowerResult => Bool.
emits-caller-equation? r: LowerResult => Bool.
helper-name-unique? n: String => Bool.

> Documentation (Patch 5).
document-kind d: Document => DocumentKind.
document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d: Document => Bool.
workstream-milestone-4-status => MilestoneStatus.
---

> SMT emission contract: recursive rules emit define-fun-rec,
> non-recursive rules emit declare-fun + universal-axiom.
all r: Rule | is-recursive? r <-> body-applies-self? r (body-of r).

all r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] |
  is-recursive? r ->
    emits-define-fun-rec? r lines and ~(emits-declare-fun? r lines).

all r: Rule, lines: [SmtLine] |
  ~(is-recursive? r) ->
    emits-declare-fun? r lines and emits-universal-axiom? r lines.

logically-equivalent? decl-fun-rec decl-fun-plus-axiom.

> Build-path contract: bare while loops construct ir1While
> unless guard is literal-true; let-then-while pairs that fail
> L3 fall through to ir1While.
all s: TSStmt |
  is-bare-while? s and ~(rejection-mentions-literal-true? (build-mutating-body s)) ->
    is-ir1-while? (build-mutating-body s).

all s: TSStmt |
  is-bare-while? s ->
    (is-rejection? (build-mutating-body s) ->
      rejection-mentions-literal-true? (build-mutating-body s)).

all s: TSStmt |
  is-let-then-while-pair? s and ~(l3-bounded-recognized? s) ->
    is-ir1-while? (build-mutating-body s).

> Lowering contract: literal-true and multi-location reject;
> successful lowering produces a fixed-point loop body
> (terminationMetric: null) plus a recursive helper rule-decl
> and a caller equation.
all s: WhileStmt |
  is-literal-true? s ->
    recognizer-rejects-literal-true? s.

all s: WhileStmt |
  mutated-count s > 1 ->
    recognizer-rejects-multi-location? s.

all s: WhileStmt, b: IR1SsaLoopBody |
  ~(is-literal-true? s) and mutated-count s = 1 ->
    null-metric? (body-metric b).

all s: WhileStmt |
  ~(is-literal-true? s) and mutated-count s = 1 ->
    emits-recursive-helper? (lower-result-of-while s) and
    emits-caller-equation? (lower-result-of-while s).

> Documentation invariants.
all d: Document |
  document-kind d = agents-md ->
    document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = ir-doc ->
    document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d.

all d: Document |
  document-kind d = workstream-doc ->
    document-mentions-l4-fixed-point? d.

workstream-milestone-4-status = landed.
